data.reasoningText:
This is an MSTest 3.8.0 project using Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) — indicated by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true`. The user wants to run tests and collect crash dumps if the test host crashes.

For .NET tests using Microsoft.Testing.Platform, crash dump collection can be enabled via the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` NuGet package. Let me set this up:

1. Add the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` package
2. Run the tests with `--crashdump` option

Let me add the package and run the tests.

index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-137f3d4b568d4e1da602ac57f82e9557/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-137f3d4b568d4e1da602ac57f82e9557/TestProject.csproj
@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
+    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump" Version="1.6.1" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
 </Project>

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag is an MTP-specific CLI argument but `dotnet test` is passing it through MSBuild which doesn't understand it. Since this project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, I need to pass it differently. Let me check how to pass MTP-specific arguments through `dotnet test`.

With MTP + `dotnet test`, you pass MTP-specific arguments after `--`. Let me try:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

data.content:
Tests passed. Here's what I did:

1. **Added `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump`** (v1.6.1) to the project — this is the MTP extension that monitors the test host and writes a crash dump if it crashes.

2. **Run command:** `dotnet test -- --crashdump`  
   The `--` separator passes `--crashdump` to the Microsoft Testing Platform runner.

All 3 tests passed. If the test host ever crashes, a `.dmp` file will be collected automatically in the test results directory. You can also use `--crashdump-type full` (or `mini`) to control the dump type.
